candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made non-circular and quantitatively testable by defining a theory-independent measure of a 'recursively usable zone of metastability' across possible laws/constants? The core unresolved tension is whether 'fine-tuning' is being redescribed in complexity language or whether there is a genuine explanatory invariant that predicts narrow viable corridors before looking at our universe.

Is the claimed narrowness of the complexity-permitting region actually generic, or does it depend sensitively on the choice of parameterization, background laws, and complexity metric? The open question is whether multiscale complexity truly occupies a small measure in theory space, or whether apparent fine-tuning is an artifact of holding too much fixed while varying a few constants.

What is the minimal physical structure required for open-ended knowledge growth, and is chemistry/star-based hierarchy only one implementation among many? This tension matters because the conjecture may overfit to familiar matter: if radically different substrates could support memory, error correction, and repeated interactions, then the proposed boundary between chaos and sterility may be much broader or differently shaped than suggested.
